findings.title: ShinyHunters Claims Odido (Dutch Telecom) Breach — Millions Affected
findings.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang claims to have breached Dutch telecom provider Odido, stealing millions of user records. Data reportedly includes personal details of subscribers.

findings.title: North Korean Lazarus Group Linked to Medusa Ransomware Attacks on US Healthcare
findings.summary: North Korean state-backed Lazarus group is actively targeting US healthcare organizations using Medusa ransomware. This represents an evolution from espionage to direct ransomware extortion by DPRK actors.

findings.title: Optimizely Data Breach via Voice Phishing (Vishing) Attack
findings.summary: New York-based ad tech company Optimizely confirmed a data breach after threat actors compromised systems through a voice phishing (vishing) attack. Undisclosed number of customers notified.

findings.title: Android Mental Health Apps (14.7M Installs) Riddled with Security Flaws
findings.summary: Multiple mental health mobile apps on Google Play with 14.7M combined downloads contain security vulnerabilities that could expose sensitive medical information of users.

findings.title: CVE-2026-1731 BeyondTrust RCE — Active Reconnaissance Observed by GreyNoise
findings.summary: GreyNoise detailed active reconnaissance for CVE-2026-1731 within 24 hours of PoC publication. A single IP accounts for 86% of scanning. Attackers probing non-standard ports, using shared tooling via VPN tunnels. Exploitation is imminent/ongoing.
findings.dedup: update — previously reported as vulnerability; now confirmed active scanning with detailed TTPs

findings.title: EnOcean SmartServer IoT — Multiple Vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-22885, CVE-2026-20761)
findings.summary: Claroty Team82 disclosed two vulnerabilities in EnOcean SmartServer IoT devices on Feb 19, 2026. These IoT/OT devices are commonly used in building automation.

findings.title: Advantest (Japanese Semiconductor Testing Giant) Hit by Ransomware
findings.summary: Advantest, which makes testing equipment for major semiconductor manufacturers, has deployed incident response protocols following a ransomware attack.

findings.title: Phobos Ransomware Affiliate Arrested in Poland
findings.summary: A Phobos ransomware affiliate has been arrested in Poland, continuing international law enforcement pressure on ransomware operators.

findings.title: Air Côte d'Ivoire Ransomware Attack
findings.summary: West African airline Air Côte d'Ivoire has been hit by a ransomware attack, with threat actors claiming to have exfiltrated data.

findings.title: Cellebrite Linked to Hacking Kenyan Political Candidate's Phone
findings.summary: Citizen Lab has linked Cellebrite's mobile forensics tools to the unauthorized access of a Kenyan political candidate's phone, raising concerns about abuse of commercial surveillance tools.

findings.title: CrowdStrike: Adversaries Moving Through Networks in Under 30 Minutes
findings.summary: CrowdStrike Global Threat Report warns adversaries are leveraging AI to make campaigns more efficient, with breakout times now under 30 minutes in many cases.

findings.title: Vanta Diagnostics (Formerly Vikor Scientific) Breach — 140K Affected
findings.summary: Vanta Diagnostics has disclosed a data breach affecting approximately 140,000 individuals, involving sensitive medical and personal data.

findings.title: OysterLoader: Multi-Stage Evasion Loader Campaign
findings.summary: Sekoia details OysterLoader (Broomstick/CleanUp), a C++ multi-stage evasion loader being used in active campaigns. Multiple evasion stages make detection challenging.

findings.title: IClickFix: Widespread WordPress ClickFix Framework
findings.summary: Sekoia documents IClickFix, a widespread WordPress-targeting framework using the ClickFix social engineering tactic to deliver malware payloads.
findings.dedup: update — extends earlier ClickFix campaign reporting with new WordPress-specific framework details

findings.title: Endor Labs Discloses Six New Vulnerabilities in OpenClaw
findings.summary: Endor Labs has published details of six new vulnerabilities in the popular AI assistant OpenClaw, expanding the attack surface for AI agent platforms.

findings.title: Bitdefender: OpenClaw Malicious Skill Trap Analysis
findings.summary: Bitdefender Labs published deep analysis of the OpenClaw malicious skill trap — where helpful-looking skills contain hidden payloads targeting AI agent users.

findings.title: LummaStealer Revival with CastleLoader
findings.summary: Bitdefender Labs details how LummaStealer is getting a second life alongside a new loader called CastleLoader, showing the infostealer ecosystem continues to evolve.

findings.title: Spain Arrests Anonymous Fenix Hacktivists for DDoS Attacks on Government Sites
findings.summary: Spanish authorities arrested four alleged hacktivists believed to have DDoSed government ministries, political parties, and public institutions.

findings.title: Cisco MCP Security Report: AI Agent Infrastructure 'Woefully Insecure'
findings.summary: Cisco warns that Model Context Protocol (MCP) — AI's 'connective tissue' for agent communication — is insecure by design, creating a broad attack surface as organizations deploy AI agents.

findings.title: Data-Only Extortion Growing as Ransomware Gangs Seek Better Profits
findings.summary: Arctic Wolf report shows data-only extortion (no encryption) is growing as threat actors find it more profitable and lower risk than traditional ransomware.

summary: Day cycle scan completed with 89/89 sources checked. 20 new findings and 2 updates. Key highlights: (1) Lazarus Group now linked to Medusa ransomware targeting US healthcare — significant escalation by DPRK actors. (2) GreyNoise details active CVE-2026-1731 reconnaissance with sophisticated tooling. (3) ShinyHunters claims major Dutch telecom breach. (4) Endor Labs + Bitdefender flag growing AI agent security concerns (OpenClaw vulns + malicious skills). (5) Cisco warns MCP infrastructure is 'woefully insecure'. (6) Multiple ransomware incidents across semiconductor (Advantest) and aviation (Air Côte d'Ivoire) sectors.
